criteria.label: Explain the difference between an experiment ID and a tag with a concrete example.
criteria.example: State that run `a3f91c2` is unique while tag `lr-sweep` can label ten runs from the same hyperparameter search.

criteria.label: Describe the basic model registry flow from training to Production.
criteria.example: Sketch: train run → tag `candidate` → register as `my-model` v4 → Staging tests → transition v4 to Production.

criteria.label: List three fields you would log on every run to support fair comparison.
criteria.example: Name git commit hash, data version or snapshot ID, and random seed as params or tags alongside validation metrics.

criteria.label: Compare two run IDs and justify which to register.
criteria.example: Given runs `abc` (val_f1=0.84, latency 120ms) and `def` (val_f1=0.83, latency 80ms), pick one with a one-sentence tradeoff rationale.
